steps.title: Find the shown leg
steps.content:
The shown segment has endpoints $(-6,4)$ and $(3,10)$, so its horizontal change is $9$ and its vertical change is $6$.
$$\sqrt{9^2+6^2}=\sqrt{117}=3\sqrt{13}$$

steps.title: Use the triangle area
steps.content:
Let the other leg have length $x$. For a right triangle,
$$36\sqrt{13}=\frac{1}{2}(3\sqrt{13})x$$

steps.title: Solve for the other leg
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $2$, then divide by $3\sqrt{13}$.
$$72\sqrt{13}=3\sqrt{13}x$$
$$x=24$$
The answer is B.
